cataloging_subject.label: Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged subject is the opening selection of Benjamin Mackerell's 1737 antiquarian account as anonymously introduced and printed by the Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society in 1852. CME00122 represents printed p. 315 through the top portion of p. 334, ending immediately after Yelverton's Mediation: a society source note, Mackerell's introduction, a Middle English charter-and-ordinance text, an English rendering of Henry V's 1417 letters patent, and the Middle English agreement known as Yelverton's Mediation. The immediate article resumes lower on p. 334 and continues through p. 374, so it is not fully encoded. This record keeps the historical documents, Mackerell's two-volume manuscript History of Norwich, the 1852 article, the journal-volume scan, the U-M encoding, and the checked-in XML distinct.

subject_statements.label: Guild of St George in Norwich
Admission, profession, visitation, discipline, and community governance

summaries.value: An edited opening selection from Benjamin Mackerell's antiquarian history of Norwich's Guild of St George. After a society source note and Mackerell's introduction, it prints guild ordinances governing worship, livery, feasting, office, discipline, poor relief, and burial; an English rendering of Henry V's 1417 incorporation and mortmain patent; and the 1452 agreement that joined the guild's leadership and membership to Norwich civic office.

CME00122 begins with the article heading on printed p. 315 and ends after Yelverton's Mediation at the top of p. 334. The source article resumes lower on p. 334 and continues through p. 374 with Mackerell's discussion, extracted ordinances, office lists, pageantry, property, and dissolution; that remainder is not encoded.

Three date layers conflict in the sources. The first printed document says the fraternity began in 1324, while the current NRO historical account says 1385. The 1852 edition closes Yelverton's Mediation with 1451, while the current NRO catalog and modern scholarship date it 27 March 1452. The inspected volume title page says 1852, while U-M's publication field says 1851 and ADS labels the issue 1849.

extent.description: The 71 modeled prose units are the XML's 71 P elements: two society headnote paragraphs, one Mackerell introduction paragraph, 44 first-charter/ordinance paragraphs, eight patent/transition paragraphs plus one tailnote paragraph, and 15 Yelverton paragraphs. The file also contains one DIV1, three DIV2, four HEAD, 21 PB, two CLOSER, one DATELINE, two DATE, one SIGNED, one TAILNOTE, four italic HI, and 65 SUP elements; it has no verse, NOTE, GAP, or UNCLEAR elements. Counts describe the encoding, not original medieval document structure.

content_parts.label: Mackerell's introduction, Of St. George's Company
content_parts.summary: Mackerell announces a full institutional history and says he copied the first charter from a company folio manuscript, Book I marked A.

locators.value: p. 315; Internet Archive PDF p. 773; BookReader n775
first DIV2 TYPE='editor's preface'; one HEAD and one P

content_parts.label: First charter and ordinances
content_parts.summary: A Middle English charter-and-ordinance text describing the fraternity, royal confirmation, annual worship and feast, liveries and procession, offices and elections, discipline, alms, sickness, burial, and admission payments, including bracketed later additions.

locators.value: pp. 316-325; Internet Archive PDF pp. 775-793; BookReader n777-n795
statement of establishment by royal charter and aims, with ordinances; exact folio range unresolved
second DIV2 TYPE='charter', direct P elements 1-44

notes: The text's 1324 foundation statement conflicts with the current NRO account's 1385 date; no silent correction is made.

content_parts.label: Henry V letters patent for the Guild of St George
content_parts.summary: Mackerell's transition followed by an English rendering of Henry V's letters patent confirming and incorporating the guild, authorizing officers, ordinances, a common seal, litigation, and property up to ten pounds yearly, dated 9 May 1417, with Wyndham signature and a Great Seal note.

locators.value: pp. 325-327; Internet Archive PDF pp. 793-797; BookReader n795-n799
royal-charter establishment statement; exact patent locus and source-book identity unresolved
second DIV2 direct P elements 45-52, two CLOSER elements, and TAILNOTE/P

notes: Mackerell says he found and transcribed this after his first transcription from a book belonging to the company, but the inspected sources do not name the translator or prove the exact modern locus.

content_parts.label: Yelverton's Mediation
content_parts.summary: The agreement joining Norwich's mayoral and common-council offices to governance and membership of the Guild of St George, regulating worship, office, property, discipline, and annual reading of the articles.

locators.value: printed p. 328 through the top of p. 334; Internet Archive PDF pp. 799-811; BookReader n801-n813
f. 189 according to Durkee 2017, p. 179 n. 44; current NRO catalog describes a copy agreement dated 27 March 1452
third DIV2 TYPE='document'; one HEAD and 15 P elements

notes: The NRO record identifies the surviving item as a copy agreement, not the original indenture.

evidence.locator: IDG, BODY/DIV1, two HEADNOTE elements, three DIV2 elements, and all descendants
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for identifiers, titles, content sequence, page references, language layers, prose form, the five modeled parts, superscript and italic representation, and exact encoded boundaries.

evidence.citation: Independent lxml structural and boundary audit of CME00122.xml against the complete 1852 volume scan.
evidence.locator: DIV1, DIV2, HEAD, HEADNOTE, P, PB, CLOSER, DATELINE, DATE, SIGNED, TAILNOTE, HI, SUP, GAP, UNCLEAR, NOTE, LG, and L counts; source-page boundary comparison
evidence.summary: Confirms 40,267 bytes; one DIV1; three DIV2; four HEAD; two HEADNOTE; 71 P; 21 PB; two CLOSER; one DATELINE; two DATE; one SIGNED; one TAILNOTE; four HI; 65 SUP; and no GAP, UNCLEAR, NOTE, LG, or L. Comparison with the scan confirms that the selection ends at the top of printed p. 334, while the article resumes lower on that page and continues through p. 374.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Account of the Company of St. George, in Norwich, CME00122.
evidence.summary: Confirms stable identity, expanded item title, Mackerell attribution, subject, table of contents, public text access, and item-level Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement; its publication field says 1851 despite the scanned volume's 1852 title page.

evidence.citation: Benjamin Mackerell, 'Account of the Company of St. George in Norwich,' from his manuscript History of Norwich (1737), Norfolk Archaeology, vol. 3 (Norwich: Norfolk and Norwich Archaeological Society, 1852), 315-374.
evidence.locator: volume title page; contents; article pp. 315-374; encoded selection p. 315 through the top of p. 334 = PDF pp. 773-811 = BookReader n775-n813
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the 1852 imprint, article boundary, anonymous society framing, Mackerell's source statements, all represented documentary text and dates, exact encoded page range, and omitted continuation.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and complete derivatives for norfolkarchaeolo03norf, Norfolk Archaeology volume 3.
evidence.summary: Confirms item identifier, volume 3, date 1852, society creator, 1,092 scanned images, and exact complete PDF/OCR derivative names, sizes, and provider checksums; it contains no explicit rights or license field.

evidence.citation: Norfolk Record Office, NNAS S2/1/1-2, Manuscript History of Norwich by Benjamin Mackerell.
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, title, Benjamin Mackerell dates, pre-1738 date, two-volume extent, NRO custody, volume-I provenance notes, and worn-spine caution; the record exposes no digital object or copy locus for the St George account.

evidence.citation: Norfolk Record Office, NCR 17b/9, Register of Guild members, assembly minutes and other memoranda.
evidence.summary: Confirms a 1441-November 1525 paper-and-parchment guild volume in original wooden boards, containing a royal-charter establishment statement and ordinances, member lists, the copy agreement of 27 March 1452, later minutes, lists, and oath; it provides no public digital object.

evidence.citation: Frank Meeres, 'Outliving St George,' Norfolk Record Office blog, 23 April 2026.
evidence.summary: Current NRO historical account dating the guild's foundation to 1385 and royal charter to 1417, identifying surviving records in NCR cases 8e-g and 17b, and outlining worship, relief, pageantry, Reformation survival, and later transformation.

evidence.citation: Dana Ann Durkee, Social Mobility and the Worsted Weavers of Norwich, c.1450-1530 (doctoral thesis, Durham University, 2017).
evidence.summary: Modern scholarly cross-check identifying Yelverton's Mediation as NRO NCR 17b/9, f. 189, dating the reform to 1452, and citing Mackerell's full 1852 article at pp. 315-374.

evidence.citation: Archaeology Data Service Library, Norfolk Archaeology 03, issue record 1180093 and article record 3236321.
evidence.summary: Confirms article pages 315-374 and provider access, but assigns the issue year 1849 and links a different Internet Archive volume object; these catalog data conflict with the inspected volume-3 title page dated 1852.

Provides the repository-to-U-M-to-1852-edition-to-Mackerell-manuscript chain, scoped links to NCR 17b/9, complete local source inventory, current access and reproduction routes, rights layers, editorial practices, and open questions.

open_questions.question: Where exactly in NRO NNAS S2/1/1-2 does Mackerell's St George account occur, and can NRO confirm that his 'Book I, marked A' is current NCR 17b/9 and identify the exact loci for all three represented documents?
next_steps: Request an NRO item-level or folio-level crosswalk for the St George account within both Mackerell volumes.
Ask whether Book I/A is NCR 17b/9 and whether the patent and first ordinances share that source with Yelverton's Mediation.
Retain Yelverton's f. 189 link separately because it has modern scholarly support; do not extend that folio to the other documents.

open_questions.question: Why does the first document printed by Mackerell date the fraternity's beginning to 1324 when the current NRO account and modern scholarship date it to 1385?
next_steps: Inspect NCR 17b/9 directly to determine the manuscript reading and documentary layer behind Mackerell's 1324 text.
Consult the 1389 guild return and modern editions before deciding whether 1324 is a scribal, transcriptional, typographical, or interpretive error.
Keep the represented text's claim distinct from the modern historical date.

open_questions.question: Can complete, foliation-stable color facsimiles of both NNAS S2/1/1-2 and NCR 17b/9 be obtained and mapped to this work?
next_steps: Use NRO's online document-order form or contact norfrec@norfolk.gov.uk. For both Mackerell volumes and NCR 17b/9, request a complete color object capture that includes front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves or endleaves, blank leaves, every manuscript leaf recto and verso, and edges if NRO offers edge photography; ask for stable image-to-folio mapping.
Ask NRO first whether complete existing digital surrogates already exist and what delivery and publication terms apply.
If images are supplied, preserve the entire delivery and rights notice, then map the Mackerell account, NCR 17b/9 f. 189, and the other source loci without cropping the cached master.
notes: The official catalog records expose no digital objects, and exact-shelfmark web searches found no legitimate public facsimile; this is dated negative evidence, not proof that NRO has no internal surrogate.

open_questions.question: Why do U-M and ADS assign 1851 and 1849 respectively when the inspected source volume's title page and Internet Archive metadata say 1852?
next_steps: Check original issue wrappers, society proceedings, and serial publication history for installment or issue dates.
Use 1852 for the inspected bound-volume citation unless issue-level evidence establishes a distinct earlier publication event.
Report the discrepancy to provider catalogs if appropriate.

notes: Mackerell's account, the three historical documentary layers, their surviving archival copies, the 1852 article, U-M encoding, and repository XML are modeled separately.
The exact checked-in XML is git blob d148227be1810385e2a1b37ea16ca254171cb4e1 at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
All counts and source-page boundaries were independently recomputed on 2026-07-13.
